category: Decaf navigation and related-product text mistaken for current coffee attribute

consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. 'coffee.roaster_country' is supported indirectly by 'Portland, Maine' implying United States. Boolean labels are consistent with the page: single-origin non-decaf filter coffee, not espresso, not a blend, and no evidence of co-ferment/infusion.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is spot-on for a Shopify-style html-to-text render: breadcrumb, title/vendor, price, size selector, add-to-cart, shipping note, specs block, descriptive copy, recommended brew methods, related products, footer, and an embedded variant data section. The voice is credible for modern specialty coffee—clean, slightly marketing-forward, with typical sensory language like 'high-definition,' 'floral acidity,' and 'layered fruit character.' Origin details are coherent: El Diviso / Nestor Lasso / Bruselas, Huila / Pink Bourbon / washed / 1,750–1,900 masl is a believable combination, and harvest timing also reads plausibly. Variant data is mostly realistic too, with sensible SKUs, matching names, and a plausible 250g-to-1kg price step. Minor tells keep it from a 5: the page visibly offers only '250g' in the main selector while the embedded variants include a 1kg option, which could happen in real exports but feels slightly inconsistent; 'careful washed fermentation' is a bit awkwardly phrased; and the product copy is a touch too neatly archetypal, as if assembled from specialty coffee page conventions. Overall, though, it is highly believable.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: Strawberry jam, Meyer lemon, raw sugar

This Pink Bourbon lot from El Diviso is the kind of clean, high-definition Colombian coffee we wait all season for. In the cup we find a saturated strawberry sweetness, bright Meyer lemon, and a soft raw-sugar finish that keeps the profile elegant rather than heavy.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Strawberry jam, Meyer lemon, raw sugar
label.coffee.producer_text: Nestor Lasso and his family cultivate small hillside parcels near Bruselas in Huila, picking selectively for ripe cherries before a careful washed fermentation and slow drying on raised beds.
